assertions.description: The gerber set correctly identifies 4 copper layers (F.Cu, B.Cu, GL2/In1.Cu, GL3/In2.Cu), 9 gerber files, and 1 drill file. Drill classification via X2 attributes works correctly: 1800 vias (0.3mm), 168 component holes (0.65mm and 1.016mm), and 32 mounting holes (3.1mm NPTH). All layers are flagged as aligned. The SMD ratio of 0.94 is reasonable for this mostly-SMD design.

The drill_classification section correctly uses X2 attributes to categorize holes. 1800 vias at 0.3mm matches the PCB's via_count of 1800 (panelized, 8 copies × 225 = 1800). 168 component holes: 16 at 0.65mm and 152 at 1.016mm correspond to the THT connectors. 32 mounting holes at 3.1mm NPTH correspond to 4 mounting holes per board × 8 boards on the panel.

The gerber component_refs list includes 'Q1' which does not appear in the schematic (the schematic has Q2 as the only transistor). This likely refers to a kibuzzard or board-only footprint in the panel that was not given a proper reference. The PCB's component_groups shows 'G' references including G1 and multiple G*** wildcards. This is a minor discrepancy between gerber-extracted component list and schematic components.

The trace_widths section identifies 5 unique widths: 0.1524mm, 0.1778mm, 0.2819mm, 0.349mm, and 0.4064mm. The minimum of 0.1524mm (6 mil) and minimum feature of 0.125mm are appropriate for a standard 4-layer PCB with typical design rules.

INCORRECT: The completeness section reports missing_required: ['Edge.Cuts'] and complete: false. However, the file SparkFun_RTK_Postcard_panelized.GKO exists and contains the board outline data (it has %TA.AperFunction,Profile*% aperture function). The GKO file's X2 TF.FileFunction header incorrectly reads '%TF.FileFunction,Soldermask,Bot*%' (which appears to be a KiCad export issue or file naming artifact), but the file content clearly contains profile/outline geometry. The analyzer should detect the Profile aperture function inside the file as Edge.Cuts layer data even when the TF.FileFunction header doesn't say Profile/BoardOutline.
